summary: Wind/sword Arena fight with two mercenary adds. Tikilen becomes much harder below 40% HP when her shield drops and non-critical hits stop shaving shields.
mechanics: Main weaknesses are bow, fan, and light. Both adds share bow/light coverage, use counters or party buffs, and can slow the fight if left alive. Tikilen follows scripted HP-band patterns and gains a critical-only shield phase at low HP.
strategy: Bring bow/light multi-hit coverage, a tank or strong mitigation, and steady P.Atk/E.Atk debuffs. Avoid feeding physical counters, remove adds before committing to Tikilen, and prepare hunters or guaranteed-crit support for the low-HP phase.

actions.title: Tikilen HP Pattern
notes: Loops from turn 1 and resumes after break. Last Stand is one-time and enables critical-only shield damage until broken.

actions.title: Skilled Mercenary
rows: Party regen after a brace turn
notes: If Tikilen has 1-6 shields, this add restores 3 shields instead, unless Last Stand is active.

actions.title: Veteran Mercenary
notes: Loops from turn 1 and resumes after break.

summary: Physical-heavy dancer fight with two dancer adds and a dangerous late phase that locks some of Glossom's weaknesses.
mechanics: Glossom starts weak to spear, axe, and dark, while the adds give useful axe/dark overlap. At low HP, spear and axe are locked, forcing dark-focused breaks or off-weakness shield tools.
strategy: Use physical defense gear, P.Atk debuffs, axe/dark coverage, and stable healing. Remove adds first so buffs and extra actions do not snowball, then plan the final break around dark damage.

actions.title: Glossom Above 50%
notes: Loops from turn 1 and resumes after break.

actions.title: Glossom Below 50%
notes: The power-up is one-time. After the brace, Destructive fan AoE is used next, then the table resumes.

summary: Element-heavy scholar fight with adds and a spiritstone that changes its weakness among Varkyn's main weaknesses.
mechanics: Varkyn is weak to sword, staff, and ice. Adds cover sword/spear/bow/tome/ice/light, and the autonomous spiritstone has an infinite shield count with a changing weakness.
strategy: Favor elemental defense, keep E.Atk debuffs active, and use sword/ice coverage to hit multiple targets. Handle adds before racing Varkyn, and do not waste turns trying to break the infinite-shield stone.

actions.title: Varkyn HP Pattern
notes: Loops from turn 1 and resumes after break. The middle 35-34% band is rarely seen. Unthinking Mind is one-time.

actions.title: Autonomous Spiritstone
rows: 3 random light hits; each can lower light resistance.
Cycles staff -> sword -> ice, then randomizes after Unthinking Mind.
Gains a permanent counter against weakness hits.
Raises threat after the weakness-counter setup.

summary: Fast dagger/lightning boss with two adds. Her second half adds pursuit hits and her final quarter increases pursuit pressure.
mechanics: Ri'tu is weak to fire, wind, and light. Adds offer wind/light overlap, but one has very narrow weaknesses. At 50% HP she starts pursuing her last target; at 25% HP pursuit becomes stronger and she speeds up.
strategy: Control speed, maintain physical mitigation, and use wind/light coverage to keep adds under control. Avoid entering the 50% and 25% thresholds without healing and debuffs already established.

actions.title: Ri'tu HP Pattern
notes: Loops from turn 1 and resumes after break. The 50% and 25% stances are each one-time threshold actions.

summary: Solo axe boss that limits multi-hit shield shaving and escalates from two actions to three actions late.
mechanics: Gertrude is weak to dagger, bow, and fire. She repeatedly applies a shield-lock effect that prevents one character's multi-hit action from shaving more than one shield. HP thresholds increase shields, change patterns, and eventually grant three actions.
strategy: Use physical-defense gear, strong P.Atk debuffs, and single-hit or follow-up shaving. Do setup on turn 1, avoid relying on multi-hit shield shaving, and prepare burst to skip the 75% shield increase if possible.

actions.title: Gertrude Above 50%
notes: Loops from turn 1 with two actions and resumes after break. Combat drug is one-time in each band.

actions.title: Gertrude Below 50%
rows: Combat drug + heal slash; then 3 actions
notes: Below 50%, break recovery restarts from turn 1. Shield-lock is re-applied at battle start and break recovery.

summary: Large multi-enemy fight against Yunnie and the Yuunagi group. Several adds have different weakness sets, so broad coverage matters.
mechanics: Yunnie is weak to sword, axe, and fan. The adds cover sword/axe/fan plus lightning, fire, ice, dagger, and staff depending on position. The fight pressures the party through many concurrent actions.
strategy: Bring wide AoE shield coverage, especially sword/axe/fan, and remove adds methodically before focusing Yunnie. Defensive stability matters more than a narrow burst plan because the encounter has many bodies.

actions.title: Yunnie HP Pattern
notes: Call actions are one-time and do not consume an action, but only happen if the relevant add wave has been defeated.

actions.title: Yuunagi Adds
notes: Several adds use a brace/self-destruct finisher when they or Yunnie cross HP thresholds.

summary: Staff martial-arts fight with weakness locks, counter states, and crow adds that join after HP thresholds.
mechanics: Yan Long is weak to sword, spear, and light, but locks and rotates countered weaknesses. Crows are weak to axe and tome. Hitting a countered locked weakness increases his next action count.
strategy: Track which weakness is locked before attacking, use safe weakness coverage, and clear crows quickly. Keep physical mitigation high and avoid giving Yan Long extra actions through careless attacks.

actions.title: Yan Long Above 50%
notes: Weakness locks/counters rotate at turn end. Counter hits add one action to the next turn.

actions.title: Yan Long Below 50%
rows: Fighting-spirit art; removes locks/counters and enables 3 actions
Front-row ice + staff res down; then 3 actions
notes: Turn-end purge changes to a debuff cleanse below 50%.

summary: Machine-heavy fight where Largo is supported by several magic machines with different weakness sets.
mechanics: Largo is weak to dagger, bow, and ice. Machines add broad but changing coverage including sword, staff, bow, fan, ice, lightning, and wind. The fight becomes dangerous if machines stay up while Largo advances his pattern.
strategy: Bring ice plus dagger/bow coverage and enough AoE to clear machines. Keep mitigation active during machine waves, then use breaks to burst Largo once the field is stable.

actions.title: Largo Below 65%
rows: Very strong spear + SP restore; braces
Very strong spear + SP restore; braces
notes: If either final machine dies, or Largo reaches 25%, he uses Boost Shoes and enters the 3-action pattern.

actions.title: Largo 3-Action Pattern
notes: Boost Shoes is one-time and consumes that turn. Break timing can defer the braced lightning shot by one action cycle.

summary: Three-enemy fight with Hammy and two assistants. Cover, evasion, traps, counters, and BP-based finishers make target order important.
mechanics: Hammy is weak to spear, fan, and wind. Her assistants can cover her, gain physical or elemental evasion, and punish the wrong damage type. HP thresholds change all enemy patterns.
strategy: Use fan/wind overlap, watch which assistant is covering, and avoid triggering trap counters. Clear the support enemies cleanly, then handle Hammy's final self-buff and shield-lock phase with planned breaks.

actions.title: Opening Pattern
notes: After Hammy Finish, restart from turn 1. Break recovery resumes from the current position.

actions.title: After 'Careless'
notes: This phase starts when one enemy drops below 75% HP.

actions.title: After 'Pinch'
notes: This phase starts when one enemy drops below 50% HP. Triggering the attack trap can heavily damage the front row.

actions.title: After 'Fight Starts Now'
notes: This phase starts when an enemy falls below 25% HP; the trap/blame type varies.

actions.title: Solo Hammy
notes: Starts after both assistants are defeated. Hammy's shield max becomes 11 and multi-hit shield lock is applied.

summary: Fan boss with normal and shadow versions. The fight revolves around Dragon Tail points, changing weaknesses, and punishing over-attacking outside break windows.
mechanics: Mirgardi begins with sword, axe, tome, and ice-style coverage, then changes weaknesses as HP falls. Dragon Tail points build when she is hit outside break and raise her threat until spent.
strategy: Do not overbuild Dragon Tail points with low-value attacks. Break decisively, burst during break, and use mitigation for fan and elemental pressure. Track the current weakness set before planning each break.

actions.title: Mirgardi Normal
notes: Turn-end tempo effect fires outside break. At 40% or lower, break recovery also triggers weakness shift.

actions.title: Mirgardi Shadow
notes: Turn-end tempo effect continues outside break. At 90% or lower, break recovery also triggers weakness shift.

summary: Spear/bow/fire/light fight with a stronger shadow version. The shadow version has very high shields and demands planned breaks.
mechanics: Base Kagemune has spear, bow, fire, and light weaknesses; the shadow version keeps the same weakness theme with much higher shield pressure. Patterns lean into physical pressure and strict break timing.
strategy: Bring redundant spear/bow/fire/light shaving, keep defensive debuffs active, and do not enter the shadow version without enough burst saved for break windows.

actions.title: Kagemune Triggers
rows: Gains 2 actions plus random-target evasion above 90%, AoE evasion from about 90-60%, and both evasion types plus offensive buffs below about 60%.
Refreshes random-target, AoE, or both evasion types based on HP band.
At or below about 90%, no clones alive
Summons two clones, or three below about 25%, and gives body/clone evasion and buffs.
Front row cannot swap and physical attacks fail; back row elemental attacks fail; Kagemune gains shield lock and refreshes evasion.
5x party dagger attack with burn, frostbite, corrosion, and self potency up.
Counters the attacker or front row and can inflict burn, frostbite, or corrosion.
Clone starts a 2-count timer, then uses a full-party status attack.
notes: Game8 lists a detailed action catalog and trigger rules, not a simple repeating turn loop for this fight.

actions.title: Kagemune Action Catalog
rows: Front-row fire plus P.Def down and burn.
3 random physical hits; each can lower P.Atk/E.Atk.
3 random wind hits; each can inflict corrosion.
3 random wind hits with dagger-res down and corrosion.
Front-row fire with P.Def/E.Def down and burn.
Full-party physical plus burn, frostbite, and corrosion for 6 turns.

summary: Modern Arena fight with a base version and a much tougher shadow version. The shadow version has huge HP and high shields.
mechanics: Base Aoi has broad weaknesses; the shadow version focuses on dagger, bow, ice, and wind with very high shield count and about ten million HP. The fight rewards stable break cycles over quick racing.
strategy: Use dagger/bow/ice/wind coverage, strong sustain, and long-fight SP planning. Save major burst for break turns and keep debuffs up through the long HP pool.

actions.title: Aoi Shadow EX Pattern
rows: Front-row attack + P.Def down; single attack
Multi-hit damage cut; elemental evasion; turn-end debuff cleanse
Front row quietude; single attack; 3 random hits
Multi-hit damage cut; physical evasion; turn-end debuff cleanse
3 random hits; 2 random hits + silence; front-row attack + P.Def down
Multi-hit damage cut; elemental evasion; turn-end debuff cleanse
Offense boosted; physical full evasion + counter
Front-row 3-hit ignore-defense attack; 10-hit mixed random attack
Offense boosted; elemental full evasion + counter
6x full-party hit + halves buffs; front-row quietude
Repeats Flowing Heaven and Cessation cycles
notes: Break recovery returns to Flowing Heaven for 4 turns while HP is green, or 2 turns while HP is yellow, then Cessation for 2 turns.

actions.title: Aoi EX2 Start
rows: Front row quietude; single attack; 3 random hits
3 random hits; 2 random hits + silence; front-row attack + P.Def down
Front-row 3-hit ignore-defense attack; 10-hit mixed random attack
6x full-party hit + halves buffs; front-row quietude
Repeats the same cycle as Shadow EX
notes: Game8 notes EX2 begins from the Shadow EX turn-3 behavior.

summary: Shorter but gimmick-heavy fight built around Silence-like ability sealing and alternating physical/elemental interception.
mechanics: Rayme is weak to spear, dagger, and wind and has about 950k HP. She opens by sealing battle abilities, intercepts all physical and elemental attacks for two turns, then alternates physical and elemental interception. Her BP max attack is dangerous.
strategy: Equip silence/special-effect countermeasures on key units, use EX skills/ultimates/latent-style tools when sealed, and avoid attacking into the active interception type. Smaller teams can reduce forced basic attacks during the opening.

actions.title: Rayme Interception
rows: Applies quietude to front and back rows for 3 turns
Use quietude immunity, EX skills, ultimates, latent-style tools, or a smaller team.
Prepare with buffs, debuffs, and regen; avoid attacks that will be nullified and countered.
Use elemental attacks or non-attacking setup.
Use physical attacks or non-attacking setup.
Expect first BP-max attack around the end of turn 4 from battle start.
notes: Interception means the attack is nullified and countered. Rayme has about 950k HP, so clean burst windows matter more than a long rotation.
